query: What's the monthly budget for u:ai and when does it reset?

queries: What's the monthly budget for u:ai and when does it reset?

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
heading_path: Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more!
retrieved.text: u:ai is operated in a secure cloud where all data is processed within the EU. The content you enter is not used for training AI models. This means that any text, documents, or questions you enter into u:ai remain in a protected environment. Bei vielen kommerziellen KI-Tools ist hingegen unklar, wo Daten gespeichert werden, wie lange sie aufbewahrt bleiben und ob sie für Trainingszwecke weiterverwendet werden. Daraus ergibt sich ein erhöhtes Risiko, insbesondere bei der Arbeit mit personenbezogenen, sensiblen oder studienrelevanten Inhalten What does this mean for me? - u:ai is compliant with data protection requirements insofar as your personal data and the information you enter are processed in accordance with the data protection standards of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). This does not apply if, within the same chat, you use the web search function or the Mistral model. - When processing the personal data of third parties, the principle of data minimisation applies as a general rule. This means that only the most essential personal data may be collected, processed and stored, in order to achive a specific defined objective. - In order to process the personal data of third parties or research data, you must obtain the consent of the persons concerned. In this context, please refer to the [information on handling research data](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/z_ctl_neu/Angebote_fuer_Studierende/Handouts__Leitfaeden_und_Ressourcen/Handouts_und_Leitfaeden/2024_09_16_AI_and_research_data_EN.pdf). - **[Sensitive data](https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGJDSrGw=/?moveToWidget=3458764658613096304&cot=14)** (this is a category of personal data that is

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: / - u:account After you have been admitted to your degree programme, the u:account for students authorises you to access the IT services of the Vienna University Computer Center (ZID). Related Links: - [u:account for students](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) - u:card (student ID) The u:card is the ID card for students of the University of Vienna. You can order your u:card in u:space under "Personal Matters" > "My u:card" as soon as your tuition fee/Student’s Union fee appears as "received on..." in u:space. You will than have to validate your u:card every semester as long as you are studying. Related Links: - [u:card – student ID](https://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [Student ID card](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31982&cHash=248afc41f9fe13a6446c039ac6c0d5db) - [Validating your u:card](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31994&cHash=eb0eb3df74edf7ac643561e8fffa6fb7) - u:find u:find is the course directory and staff/unit directory of the University of Vienna. In u:find you can find all courses offered (sorted by semesters including detailed information about the content and course instructor, place and time, registration deadlines etc.) as well as organisational units and staff of the University of Vienna. You can - search for courses, exams, persons or organisational units - view course and exam dates - register for courses and exams during the registration period (forwarding to u:space) - export a calendar - view the area map of the University of Vienna via Google Maps The course directory is being continuously updated at the beginning of the semester. Links: - [u:find -

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai has a modular structure. Not every module offers the same functions (e.g., uploads or Internet access). Depending on the task, you must select the appropriate module:

- Chatbot: General chat function with image analysis and internet search capabilities
- Image generator: Tool for generating images from text descriptions
- Translator: Translating text and files in the same layout
- Document analysis: Tool for analyzing written content from documents and option for Internet research
- Laboratory: Experimental environment for testing functions and models

#

| Chatbot | Document analysis | Laboratory | Image generator | Translator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Upload images and screenshots in chat to chat about image content | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files | No uploading of documents | Prompt limited to 4000 characters Created images cannot be customized by entering additional descriptions | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (max 40 MB per file, max 250 MB total) |
| Internet research | Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research |
| No uploading of documents | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of reference images | No uploading of images |

You do not have to submit a separate application for remission.

retrieved.title: Maintaining independence: AI-generated or AI-supported?
heading_path: Controlling AI in a targeted manner and maintaining independence
retrieved.text:
The quality of AI-generated output depends to a large extent on how precisely and reflectively a request is formulated. AI tools do not independently respond to academic requirements; rather, they orient themselves to the specifications they receive through [prompts](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/controlling-ai-in-a-targeted-manner-the-importance-of-prompting-correctly/).

It is important to define the objective, context and framework conditions clearly, as this will ensure the most effective use of AI as a supportive tool. Through targeted prompting, the user retains control over the working process, determines the direction and depth of responses, and ensures that substantive decisions remain theirs. AI thus provides impulses – the academic contextualisation and further development remain the responsibility of the user.

Formulieren Sie Prompts so, dass die KI **nicht für Sie denkt oder schreibt**, sondern Sie beim eigenen Denken unterstützt. Bitten Sie um **Fragen, Alternativen, Rückmeldungen oder Strukturvorschläge**, nicht um fertige Texte oder inhaltliche Entscheidungen. Bewährt haben sich Prompts, die:

- die KI auf eine **unterstützende Rolle** festlegen (zB Feedback, Reflexionshilfe),
- klar benennen, **was die KI nicht tun soll** (zB keine fertigen Argumente),
- deutlich machen, dass **Auswahl, Bewertung und Weiterverarbeitung** bei Ihnen liegen.

**Dabei gilt:** Die Vorschläge dienen als Ausgangspunkt und müssen fachlich geprüft, eingeordnet und weiterentwickelt werden.

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

It has to directly get in touch with the Raiffeisenbank (bank connection of the University of Vienna). A return remittance is only possible via the Raiffeisenbank. → Solution: Directly contact your bank. - An error occurred during the payment of my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. What can I do? In case of an incorrect payment the fee will be returned to your account in most cases. You have to pay again correctly and within the payment period. Then you can re-enrol in your degree programme. You can find possible reasons for an incorrect payment and solutions for the problem when you look at the question "My fee does not register as received..." - I have already paid my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Why can't I download my student documents? If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee via payment form or online banking the processing may take up to seven days. You can check when your payment is registered at the University of Vienna at u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. As soon as the

retrieved.title: Using AI in Your Studies
retrieved.text: Imagine you are tasked with writing an academic paper. You are aware of the importance of a clear research question, thorough research and a coherent line of argument. Yet instead of doing everything yourself, there is a new option: AI-supported tools can suggest literature, generate outlines, draft opening sentences and improve your writing style. Artificial intelligence has long become part of everyday student life. At the same time, its use in degree programmes raises important questions: What is permitted? Where are the boundaries? And what do students need to be aware of? On these pages, you will find concise and reliable information on the responsible use of AI in degree programmes at the University of Vienna. You will learn under which conditions AI may be used, which specific provisions apply to examinations and what requires particular attention when using AI. The content is based on the current regulations of the University of Vienna and is intended for students of all disciplines. The aim is to support a responsible, transparent and reflective use of AI. [### What Is Artificial Intelligence?](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/) [### AI in Your Studies: What Is Permitted?](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/) [### Academic work and writing](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/) [### AI and examinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-and-exams/) [## u:ai – The University of Vienna's AI tool](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/uai-the-university-of-viennas-ai-tool/) [### Further information](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/further-information/) Generative AI (OpenAI, ChatGPT 5.2) was used on this website and its subpages to adapt content for the target audience. It was

retrieved.title: Maintaining independence: AI-generated or AI-supported?
retrieved.text: revise the language of your own texts. It is imperative that you critically examine, develop and embed AI-generated results within your own line of argument. The more actively you guide AI and the more decisions you take yourself, the clearer your independent contribution remains.

retrieved.title: Basic framework for AI use
retrieved.text:
The use of AI in degree programmes is not generally prohibited, but nor is it permitted without any restrictions. Whether and how you may use AI always depends on the specific context, such as:

- the course type
- the examination or assessment format
- the specific requirements set by the lecturer
- the respective learning objectives
- the [principles of good academic practice](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/good-scientific-practice/#c1269035)

As a general rule, you are responsible for all work that you submit, including any that has been created using AI tools. This implies that:

- You are responsible for the accuracy, quality and traceability of your work.
- Any incorrect statements, distortions or fabricated sources are your responsibility.
- While AI may support you in your work, the substantive contribution and independent thinking must be your own.

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text: / - Step 1: Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee At the beginning of the semester, you have to pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee. You can check whether your payment was successful in the study overview on u:space. Once you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you are admitted for the current semester and can register for courses. [Information about tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) - Step 2: Validate your u:card As soon as you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you can validate your u:card – the University of Vienna’s student ID card – at one of the u:card terminals. Your u:card is then valid for the duration of the current semester. [u:card website](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) - Step 3: Plan your schedule The website on the [University of Vienna's range of degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) is a good starting point for planning your schedule. In addition to information about your degree programme, you can also find a link to the curriculum there. The curriculum outlines what achievements you have to complete during your degree programme. With the help of the [semester planner in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/semesterplaner), you can plan your courses and exams for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively,

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. - Only ever use your latest unique payment reference when paying. Otherwise, we are not able to correctly allocate your payment. Your unique payment reference has 12 digits and consists of numbers and letters. You can find all data you need for the payment in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Your name, student ID number and semester are not relevant. If you pay via eps-payment or credit card via u:space all required data is already filled in. Do not change it. - Do not save your unique payment reference. The data changes each semester. - You can only pay within the payment period of the respective semester. Thereby you re-enrol in your studies. We recommend: Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the beginning of the payment period. Thereby you have the opportunity to act in good time, in case of an incorrect payment. - If the prescribed fee is not received at the University of Vienna in due time, your studies will be closed.

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
retrieved.text: - for the summer semester: until the following 30 September - for the winter semester: until the following 28/29 February / - Reasons for reimbursement - Closing the degree programme before the beginning of the semester (withdrawing from a degree programme) You have [closed your degree programme](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) before the beginning of the semester? If you are not admitted to any other degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at another university, the tuition fee can be reimbursed. - Completing a degree programme before the end of the admission period (graduating) You have completed your degree programme or stage of the degree programme at the University of Vienna before the end of the admission period (date of the last examination)? If you are neither admitted to another degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at the University of Vienna nor any other Austrian university, the tuition fee can be reimbursed. - Closing the degree programme before the end of the admission period You have closed your degree programme before the end of the admission period of the current semester and you are not admitted to another degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text:
Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester. Thereby you automatically re-enrol in your studies. If you have already paid at another Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your degree programme yourself.

- You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period.
- [How much you have to pay](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and type of studies.
- Pay the exact fee (neither too much nor too little). You can find the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
- Wait until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Then you can download your student documents in u:space and validate your student ID, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).
- If you pay at another Austrian university of university of applied sciences, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself via u:space each semester.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
u:ai has a modular structure. Not every module offers the same functions (e.g., uploads or Internet access). Depending on the task, you must select the appropriate module:

- Chatbot: General chat function with image analysis and internet search capabilities
- Image generator: Tool for generating images from text descriptions
- Translator: Translating text and files in the same layout
- Document analysis: Tool for analyzing written content from documents and option for Internet research
- Laboratory: Experimental environment for testing functions and models

#

| Chatbot | Document analysis | Laboratory | Image generator | Translator |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Upload images and screenshots in chat to chat about image content | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files | No uploading of documents | Prompt limited to 4000 characters Created images cannot be customized by entering additional descriptions | Upload PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files (max 40 MB per file, max 250 MB total) |
| Internet research | Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research | No Internet research |
| No uploading of documents | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of images and screenshots | No uploading of reference images | No uploading of images |

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: / - How do I know, how much I have to pay? You can see how much you have to pay in the current semester in u:space at Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. The amount of the tuition fee depends on your citizenship. There are also differences between degree programmes and non-degree programmes. - [Detailed info about the amount of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/amount-tuition-fee/) - Where can I check, if I have successfully paid or re-enroled in my degree programme? This is how you can check if your payment was successful and you have re-enroled in your studies at the University of Vienna: 1. You can find your student documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space at Personal matters > My documents. Choose the current semester at "semester-specific documents". 2. The status of the fee in u:space > Financial matters is "received"- 3. Choose your degree programme in u:space > Studies > Study overview. There you can see the following text with a green check mark: "Re-enrolled. You are re-enrolled for your degree programme for summer/winter semester 20xx (valid until xx.xx.xxxx)". - I am completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme

reranked.title: Remission and reimbursement
reranked.text: automatically after a few days. Only pay the Students' Union fee then. [You can find further information in the Intranet of the University of Vienna (login required).](https://wiki.univie.ac.at/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=400589235) - Students with Ukrainian citizenship The tuition fee for winter semester 2026/27 is automatically remitted for students with Ukrainian citizenship who are admitted to a degree or non-degree programme. You do not have to submit a separate application for remission.

reranked.title: Maintaining independence: AI-generated or AI-supported?
heading_path: Controlling AI in a targeted manner and maintaining independence
reranked.text:
The quality of AI-generated output depends to a large extent on how precisely and reflectively a request is formulated. AI tools do not independently respond to academic requirements; rather, they orient themselves to the specifications they receive through [prompts](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/controlling-ai-in-a-targeted-manner-the-importance-of-prompting-correctly/).

It is important to define the objective, context and framework conditions clearly, as this will ensure the most effective use of AI as a supportive tool. Through targeted prompting, the user retains control over the working process, determines the direction and depth of responses, and ensures that substantive decisions remain theirs. AI thus provides impulses – the academic contextualisation and further development remain the responsibility of the user.

Formulieren Sie Prompts so, dass die KI **nicht für Sie denkt oder schreibt**, sondern Sie beim eigenen Denken unterstützt. Bitten Sie um **Fragen, Alternativen, Rückmeldungen oder Strukturvorschläge**, nicht um fertige Texte oder inhaltliche Entscheidungen. Bewährt haben sich Prompts, die:

- die KI auf eine **unterstützende Rolle** festlegen (zB Feedback, Reflexionshilfe),
- klar benennen, **was die KI nicht tun soll** (zB keine fertigen Argumente),
- deutlich machen, dass **Auswahl, Bewertung und Weiterverarbeitung** bei Ihnen liegen.

**Dabei gilt:** Die Vorschläge dienen als Ausgangspunkt und müssen fachlich geprüft, eingeordnet und weiterentwickelt werden.

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

You have paid via eps-payment and the fee was deducted twice by the bank. Please contact your bank. It has to directly get in touch with the Raiffeisenbank (bank connection of the University of Vienna). A return remittance is only possible via the Raiffeisenbank. → Solution: Directly contact your bank. - An error occurred during the payment of my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. What can I do? In case of an incorrect payment the fee will be returned to your account in most cases. You have to pay again correctly and within the payment period. Then you can re-enrol in your degree programme. You can find possible reasons for an incorrect payment and solutions for the problem when you look at the question "My fee does not register as received..." - I have already paid my tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Why can't I download my student documents? If you have paid the tuition fee/Students' Union fee via payment form or online banking the processing may take up to seven days. You can check when your payment is registered at the University of Vienna at u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. As soon as the

Make sure to enter the latest unique payment reference in the correct field. 4. You have completed the payment form correctly. Nevertheless an error occurred. Otherwise make sure to enter the correct data. 5. You have paid in cash. If you pay in cash at a bank the payment reference is frequently not forwarded. reranked.title: Using AI in Your Studies
reranked.text: Imagine you are tasked with writing an academic paper. You are aware of the importance of a clear research question, thorough research and a coherent line of argument. Yet instead of doing everything yourself, there is a new option: AI-supported tools can suggest literature, generate outlines, draft opening sentences and improve your writing style. Artificial intelligence has long become part of everyday student life. At the same time, its use in degree programmes raises important questions: What is permitted? Where are the boundaries? And what do students need to be aware of? On these pages, you will find concise and reliable information on the responsible use of AI in degree programmes at the University of Vienna. You will learn under which conditions AI may be used, which specific provisions apply to examinations and what requires particular attention when using AI. The content is based on the current regulations of the University of Vienna and is intended for students of all disciplines. The aim is to support a responsible, transparent and reflective use of AI. [### What Is Artificial Intelligence?](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/) [### AI in Your Studies: What Is Permitted?](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/) [### Academic work and writing](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/) [### AI and examinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-and-exams/) [## u:ai – The University of Vienna's AI tool](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/uai-the-university-of-viennas-ai-tool/) [### Further information](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/further-information/) Generative AI (OpenAI, ChatGPT 5.2) was used on this website and its subpages to adapt content for the target audience. It was

reranked.title: Maintaining independence: AI-generated or AI-supported?
reranked.text: revise the language of your own texts. It is imperative that you critically examine, develop and embed AI-generated results within your own line of argument. The more actively you guide AI and the more decisions you take yourself, the clearer your independent contribution remains.

reranked.title: Basic framework for AI use
reranked.text:
The use of AI in degree programmes is not generally prohibited, but nor is it permitted without any restrictions. Whether and how you may use AI always depends on the specific context, such as:

- the course type
- the examination or assessment format
- the specific requirements set by the lecturer
- the respective learning objectives
- the [principles of good academic practice](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/academic-work-and-writing-with-ai/good-scientific-practice/#c1269035)

As a general rule, you are responsible for all work that you submit, including any that has been created using AI tools. This implies that:

- You are responsible for the accuracy, quality and traceability of your work.
- Any incorrect statements, distortions or fabricated sources are your responsibility.
- While AI may support you in your work, the substantive contribution and independent thinking must be your own.

reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text: / - Step 1: Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee At the beginning of the semester, you have to pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee. You can check whether your payment was successful in the study overview on u:space. Once you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you are admitted for the current semester and can register for courses. [Information about tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) - Step 2: Validate your u:card As soon as you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you can validate your u:card – the University of Vienna’s student ID card – at one of the u:card terminals. Your u:card is then valid for the duration of the current semester. [u:card website](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) - Step 3: Plan your schedule The website on the [University of Vienna's range of degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) is a good starting point for planning your schedule. In addition to information about your degree programme, you can also find a link to the curriculum there. The curriculum outlines what achievements you have to complete during your degree programme. With the help of the [semester planner in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/semesterplaner), you can plan your courses and exams for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively,

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: of Vienna? - Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period. Do not make any partial payments. Make sure too neither pay too little nor too much. If you pay too little, your degree programme will be automatically closed after the end of the payment period. - Only ever use your latest unique payment reference when paying. Otherwise, we are not able to correctly allocate your payment. Your unique payment reference has 12 digits and consists of numbers and letters. You can find all data you need for the payment in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Your name, student ID number and semester are not relevant. If you pay via eps-payment or credit card via u:space all required data is already filled in. Do not change it. - Do not save your unique payment reference. The data changes each semester. - You can only pay within the payment period of the respective semester. Thereby you re-enrol in your studies. We recommend: Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the beginning of the payment period. Thereby you have the opportunity to act in good time, in case of an incorrect payment. - If the prescribed fee is not received at the University of Vienna in due time, your studies will be closed.

reranked.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
reranked.text: - for the summer semester: until the following 30 September - for the winter semester: until the following 28/29 February / - Reasons for reimbursement - Closing the degree programme before the beginning of the semester (withdrawing from a degree programme) You have [closed your degree programme](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) before the beginning of the semester? If you are not admitted to any other degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at another university, the tuition fee can be reimbursed. - Completing a degree programme before the end of the admission period (graduating) You have completed your degree programme or stage of the degree programme at the University of Vienna before the end of the admission period (date of the last examination)? If you are neither admitted to another degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at the University of Vienna nor any other Austrian university, the tuition fee can be reimbursed. - Closing the degree programme before the end of the admission period You have closed your degree programme before the end of the admission period of the current semester and you are not admitted to another degree programme where the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text:
Pay the prescribed tuition fee/Students' Union fee each semester. Thereby you automatically re-enrol in your studies. If you have already paid at another Austrian university, you have to re-enrol in your degree programme yourself.

- You can only pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee within the payment period.
- [How much you have to pay](/en/tuition-fee/paymentre-enrolment/) depends on your citizenship, duration of studies and type of studies.
- Pay the exact fee (neither too much nor too little). You can find the prescribed fee in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
- Wait until the fee has been received at the University of Vienna. Then you can download your student documents in u:space and validate your student ID, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).
- If you pay at another Austrian university of university of applied sciences, you have to re-enrol in your studies at the University of Vienna yourself via u:space each semester.

- winter semester 2026/27:
  from 13 July to 31 October 2026
- summer semester 2027:
  from 11 January to 31 March 2027

selected.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
selected.text:
To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

selected.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
selected.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

selected.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
heading_path: Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more!
selected.text: u:ai is operated in a secure cloud where all data is processed within the EU. The content you enter is not used for training AI models. This means that any text, documents, or questions you enter into u:ai remain in a protected environment. Bei vielen kommerziellen KI-Tools ist hingegen unklar, wo Daten gespeichert werden, wie lange sie aufbewahrt bleiben und ob sie für Trainingszwecke weiterverwendet werden. Daraus ergibt sich ein erhöhtes Risiko, insbesondere bei der Arbeit mit personenbezogenen, sensiblen oder studienrelevanten Inhalten What does this mean for me? - u:ai is compliant with data protection requirements insofar as your personal data and the information you enter are processed in accordance with the data protection standards of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). This does not apply if, within the same chat, you use the web search function or the Mistral model. - When processing the personal data of third parties, the principle of data minimisation applies as a general rule. This means that only the most essential personal data may be collected, processed and stored, in order to achive a specific defined objective. - In order to process the personal data of third parties or research data, you must obtain the consent of the persons concerned. In this context, please refer to the [information on handling research data](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/z_ctl_neu/Angebote_fuer_Studierende/Handouts__Leitfaeden_und_Ressourcen/Handouts_und_Leitfaeden/2024_09_16_AI_and_research_data_EN.pdf). - **[Sensitive data](https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGJDSrGw=/?moveToWidget=3458764658613096304&cot=14)** (this is a category of personal data that is

selected.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
selected.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

This does not apply if, within the same chat, you use the web search function or the Mistral model. - When processing the personal data of third parties, the principle of data minimisation applies as a general rule. This means that only the most essential personal data may be collected, processed and stored, in order to achive a specific defined objective. - In order to process the personal data of third parties or research data, you must obtain the consent of the persons concerned. In this context, please refer to the [information on handling research data](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/z_ctl_neu/Angebote_fuer_Studierende/Handouts__Leitfaeden_und_Ressourcen/Handouts_und_Leitfaeden/2024_09_16_AI_and_research_data_EN.pdf). - **[Sensitive data](https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGJDSrGw=/?moveToWidget=3458764658613096304&cot=14)** (this is a category of personal data that is

[4] u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

[5] u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna — Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more! > Datenschutz
data of third parties or research data, you must obtain the consent of the persons concerned. In this context, please refer to the [information on handling research data](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/z_ctl_neu/Angebote_fuer_Studierende/Handouts__Leitfaeden_und_Ressourcen/Handouts_und_Leitfaeden/2024_09_16_AI_and_research_data_EN.pdf). - **[Sensitive data](https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGJDSrGw=/?moveToWidget=3458764658613096304&cot=14)** (this is a category of personal data that is particularly sensitive, such as health data) may not be entered into u:ai without the consent of the persons concerned. - Further information on data protection can be found [here](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/ai-in-your-studies-what-is-permitted/legal-requirements-data-protection-and-copyright/#c1269046). - The University of Vienna’s data protection declaration is available at this [link](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/en/data-protection-declaration/).

[6] ABC of terminology — U
/ - u:account After you have been admitted to your degree programme, the u:account for students authorises you to access the IT services of the Vienna University Computer Center (ZID). Related Links: - [u:account for students](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) - u:card (student ID) The u:card is the ID card for students of the University of Vienna. You can order your u:card in u:space under "Personal Matters" > "My u:card" as soon as your tuition fee/Student’s Union fee appears as "received on..." in u:space. You will than have to validate your u:card every semester as long as you are studying. Related Links: - [u:card – student ID](https://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [Student ID card](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31982&cHash=248afc41f9fe13a6446c039ac6c0d5db) - [Validating your u:card](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31994&cHash=eb0eb3df74edf7ac643561e8fffa6fb7) - u:find u:find is the course directory and staff/unit directory of the University of Vienna. In u:find you can find all courses offered (sorted by semesters including detailed information about the content and course instructor, place and time, registration deadlines etc.) as well as organisational units and staff of the University of Vienna. You can - search for courses, exams, persons or organisational units - view course and exam dates - register for courses and exams during the registration period (forwarding to u:space) - export a calendar - view the area map of the University of Vienna via Google Maps The course directory is being continuously updated at the beginning of the semester. Links: - [u:find -

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna — Access and general conditions
To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

[2] u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

[3] u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna — Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more! > Datenschutz
u:ai is operated in a secure cloud where all data is processed within the EU. The content you enter is not used for training AI models. This means that any text, documents, or questions you enter into u:ai remain in a protected environment.
